description: Contract + behavioral-track invariant/property ledger. Scope: docs/contracts/*.md (generic engine contracts) + src/flows/*/contract.md (per-flow contracts) + specs/behavioral/*.md. Out of scope: CLAUDE.md hard invariants, specs/evidence.md invariants, ADR-level invariants.

enforcement_state_semantics.test-enforced: Invariant id appears as a token in at least one binding_ref test-file path (describe/it title or assertion message). Eligibility: required default for any contract invariant stated as 'MUST reject', 'MUST enforce', or 'is rejected'.
enforcement_state_semantics.audit-only: Invariant id appears as a token in scripts/audit.mjs. Eligibility: audit-layer or discipline invariants (session hygiene, change_kind discipline, citation rule).
enforcement_state_semantics.static-anchor: Invariant enforced structurally by schema export or superRefine; binding_refs name the schema file plus at least one test file that parses that schema. Eligibility: structural invariants whose id may not appear as a test title but whose enforcement cannot regress without the schema change firing a named test failure. Grandfathered scope_ids entries in tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts count as binding_refs for step.md and flow.md.
enforcement_state_semantics.prose-only: Policy statement not directly testable (e.g. when the challenger should be invoked; what the challenger cannot replace). Eligibility: behavioral-track meta-invariants only. Requires `rationale` field. A contract 'MUST reject' invariant cannot be prose-only.
enforcement_state_semantics.stage2-property: Reserved for Stage 2 property-test harness; not enforced at Stage 1 verify time. Requires `target_slice` (29 = property registry scaffold) + `reopen_condition`.
enforcement_state_semantics.blocked: Invariant declared as normative; enforcement deferred to a substrate-widening slice named in the arc via `substrate_slice:`. REQUIRES full escrow: `substrate_slice`, `owner`, `expiry_date`, `reopen_condition`, `acceptance_evidence` (post-resolution). Forbids arc close while unresolved (plan-lint rule #22). Differs from `stage2-property` in that `stage2-property` has a known harness landing at a specific Stage 2 property-scaffold slice (scheduled, non-escrow), while `blocked` requires substrate widening of a new step kind / schema / runtime surface AND an explicit expiry + reopen condition. Added at Slice 59 of planning-readiness-meta-arc per ADR-0010 §Vocabulary + §3.E (Codex pass 02 HIGH 5 fold-in).

out_of_scope_notes: AR-H1 persisted-shape binding for invariants to reports is deferred to Slice 26a. Optional `report_ids` ledger field may be added in a later slice; absence here is intentional and not a silent omission.

CLAUDE.md §Hard invariants 1-10, specs/evidence.md Invariant 1-N, and ADR-0001/0002/0003 reopen/authority constraints are out of scope for Slice 25 per Codex challenger MED #9 fold-in disposition; expansion is v0.2 scope.

invariants.rationale: Unique step ids enforced by CompiledFlow superRefine in src/schemas/compiled-flow.ts. Slice 27 flow.md v0.2 exits the skeleton grandfather and binds WF-I1 to a per-invariant titled negative test.
Closed entry_modes.start_at references enforced by CompiledFlow superRefine. Slice 27 upgrade from static-anchor to test-enforced via per-invariant titled negative test.
Closed stage step references enforced by CompiledFlow superRefine. Slice 27 upgrade from static-anchor to test-enforced via per-invariant titled negative test.
Closed route targets enforced by CompiledFlow superRefine. Slice 27 upgrade from static-anchor to test-enforced via per-invariant titled negative test.
Unique entry-mode names enforced by CompiledFlow superRefine. Slice 27 upgrade adds per-invariant titled negative test.
Unique stage ids enforced by CompiledFlow superRefine. Slice 27 upgrade adds per-invariant titled negative test.

schema_version=2 enforced by the CompiledFlow literal field on CompiledFlowBody (before superRefine). Slice 27 upgrade adds per-invariant titled negative test rejecting non-'2' schema_version values (Codex challenger LOW #8 fold-in — prior phrasing said 'surplus unknown literals' which conflated literal-mismatch with strict-key rejection).

Terminal reachability enforced by CompiledFlow superRefine via iterative fixpoint over terminal-reaching steps. New in Slice 27 flow.md v0.2 — structural requirement for runtime-proof: a flow fixture where some step cannot reach @complete/@stop/@escalate/@handoff would bootstrap a Run that could never emit run.closed.

No dead steps enforced by CompiledFlow superRefine via BFS from every entry_mode.start_at. New in Slice 27 flow.md v0.2 — a declared step not reachable from any entry is a silent declaration bug for runtime-proof fixtures. The reachability pass is checkd on WF-I1/WF-I4/WF-I2 structural soundness (allEntryStartsKnown precondition) so a single WF-I2 violation does not cascade into WF-I9 noise (Codex challenger MED #4 fold-in).

Pass-route presence enforced by CompiledFlow superRefine — every step's routes map must contain key 'pass'. CheckEvaluatedTraceEntry.outcome is z.enum(['pass','fail']) uniformly across all three check kinds (src/schemas/trace-entry.ts), so a successful check outcome routes through routes['pass']. New in Slice 27 flow.md v0.2 as Codex challenger HIGH #1 fold-in — closes the WF-I8 graph-reachable-but-runtime-unselectable hole (fixture routes like { success: '@complete' } would satisfy WF-I8 and stall at runtime). `fail`-route presence deferred to v0.3 / Stage 2.

Pass-route terminal reachability enforced by CompiledFlow superRefine by following only routes.pass. WF-I8 remains the broad terminal-reachability graph sanity check, but the current runner follows pass routes after successful checks; a flow whose fail route reaches @complete while pass cycles forever is rejected at parse time, with a runtime guard closing schema-bypass cases as aborted runs.

Test enforcement removed in the methodology strip (commit 60b1263, 2026-04-25). The cross-model challenger discipline was demoted to optional per AGENTS.md rule #7 — no longer mandatory, no longer a machine-enforceable surface. Behavioral discipline preserved as documentation.

Policy statement about when the challenger is invoked (ratchet-changing surfaces). Judgment call by operator; no machine-enforceable surface. Permitted prose-only per behavioral-track eligibility rule.

Test enforcement removed in the methodology strip (commit 60b1263, 2026-04-25). Specs/reviews/ recording machinery was stripped alongside; the challenger-pass recording discipline survives as guidance in specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md but has no machine-enforceable surface today.

Test enforcement removed in the methodology strip (commit 60b1263, 2026-04-25). Fold-in disposition discipline (Incorporated / Scoped / Rejected with rationale) was tied to the specs/reviews/ recording surface that was stripped. Discipline preserved as documentation; no machine-enforceable surface remains.

Test enforcement removed in the methodology strip (commit 60b1263, 2026-04-25). Relay-via-/codex-skill discipline is a runtime invocation choice with no testable report in the codebase; preserved as documentation in user memory + specs/behavioral/cross-model-challenger.md.

Policy statement about what the challenger MAY NOT replace (authority mapping / reference evidence / fixture parity / differential tests / state-machine tests / migration rehearsal). Enforces through methodology discipline, not a schema surface. Permitted prose-only per behavioral-track eligibility rule.

Test enforcement removed in the methodology strip (commit 60b1263, 2026-04-25). The catalog-compiler architecture this invariant targeted was not adopted; circuit-next solved prose/YAML drift through the schematic → command.md emit pattern enforced by `npm run check-flow-drift`. Original invariant preserved as documentation of the source-of-truth principle.

Test enforcement removed in the methodology strip (commit 60b1263, 2026-04-25). The catalog-compiler round-trip surface this invariant targeted was not adopted; circuit-next's emit/drift-check pipeline serves the equivalent role for command.md. Original invariant preserved as documentation.

Policy statement about compiler direction authorship (prose → YAML is hand-authored; YAML → prose is compiler-owned). The catalog compiler has not landed at v0.1; no round-trip surface exists yet. The SENTINEL test in tests/contracts/prose-yaml-parity.test.ts fires when compiler markers appear, at which point PROSE-YAML-I3 is re-classified. Permitted prose-only with target-promotion trigger.

Test enforcement removed in the methodology strip (commit 60b1263, 2026-04-25). The single-family marker / Circuit-as-justification anti-pattern this invariant guarded against was specific to the pre-strip architecture; no equivalent surface exists in circuit-next today. Preserved as documentation.

Test enforcement removed in the methodology strip (commit 60b1263, 2026-04-25). The session-lifecycle hook ceremony (SessionStart/SessionEnd/Stop) was deliberately stripped from the harness; this invariant has no machine-enforceable surface in circuit-next. Preserved as documentation.
Test enforcement removed in the methodology strip (commit 60b1263, 2026-04-25). Session-hook ceremony was stripped; no machine-enforceable surface remains. Preserved as documentation.

properties.rationale: Property test exercises the RunRelativePath Zod scalar across ~400 deterministic cases, varying along five disallowed-form axes (absolute prefix, backslash, drive-letter colon, '.'/'..'/empty segment, empty string) plus a sound-path bucket. Each axis has its own anti-vacuity floor so a silent generator regression would fail loudly. FU-T12.

Property test drives surplus-key injection across the four step variants with declared writes records (compose, verification, relay, sub-run) over ~200 cases. Each variant has accept-and-reject anti-vacuity floors so the .strict() shape is exercised end-to-end. FU-T12.

Property test exercises the WF-I2 closure: entry_modes[].start_at must resolve to a declared step id. Generator emits ~200 cases per branch via mulberry32, isolating this dimension while the chain base keeps every other flow closure invariant satisfied. FU-T12.

Property test exercises the WF-I9 reachability law: every declared step must be reachable from some entry_mode.start_at via the routes graph. Generator pairs a chainBase (sound) with an injected orphan step (declared, in-stage, but no edge points at it) to drive ~200 cases per branch. FU-T12.

Property test exercises the WF-I3 closure: every entry of stage.steps[] must resolve to a declared step id. Generator drives ~200 chainBase cases with optional ghost step id injected into stage.steps. FU-T12.

Property test exercises the WF-I4 closure: every pass-route target must be either a terminal label (@complete/@stop/@escalate/@handoff) or a declared step id. Generator drives ~200 chainBase cases with an optional unknown route target injected per case. FU-T12.

Property test exercises the WF-I8 reachability law: every declared step must reach some terminal route target via its pass-route chain. Generator drives ~200 chainBase cases, optionally introducing a self-loop on a non-last step (which both severs WF-I8 and trips WF-I11 — the assertion accepts either citation). FU-T12.
